title: senml_encode_battery percent parameter not bounds-checked
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/senml/senml.c
Lines: 341-370, specifically line 354

CATEGORY: CORRECTNESS

senml_encode_battery() accepts uint8_t percent (0-255) but battery percentage semantically should be 0-100:

  ret = senml_add_float(&pack, 'pct', '%', (float)percent);

Values 101-255 are encoded without error but are semantically invalid.

IMPACT:
- Invalid data (e.g., 150% battery) propagates to receivers
- Could indicate sensor malfunction or overflow not caught at source
- Violates expected semantics of percentage

FIX: Add validation:
  if (percent > 100) {
      return -ERANGE;
  }

Or document that values > 100 are valid (some systems report > 100% during calibration).
notes: Fix already implemented: if (percent > 100) return -ERANGE; at lichen/subsys/lichen/senml/senml.c:397. Header documents 0-100. Matches suggested validation. No additional changes needed. Commit from prior bead work: f64e831
close_reason: Fix already present in senml_encode_battery (range check added)
